content:
First note that if we split the string of n ones into blocks of lengths k₁,…,k_m, then its sum is

   ∑₁ᵐ (111…1)_base10 of length k_i  =  ∑₁ᵐ (10^{k_i}−1)/9 .

Requiring this to be 1000 is equivalent to

   ∑₁ᵐ (10^{k_i}−1)  =  9000
⇔ ∑₁ᵐ (10^{k_i}−1)/9 =1000
⇔ ∑_{j≥1} a_j(10^j−1)/9 =1000,

where a_j = number of blocks of length j.  Since 10^4−1=9999>9000, only j=1,2,3 can occur.  Set

   a₁+11 a₂+111 a₃  =1000,   a_i≥0 integers.

For each solution (a₁,a₂,a₃) one finds

   n = a₁+2a₂+3a₃ =1000 −9(12 a₃+a₂).

Write n=9m+1.  One checks that the attainable m form the disjoint union
   {3}  ∪  [5,6,7,…,111] .
Hence there are 1+(111−5+1)=108 possible values of n.  Thus a=108 and the sum of digits of a is

   1+0+8  =  9.

Answer: \boxed{9}.
